libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Generate tests against the Spring 6.0.x API surface and avoid removed Spring 5 remoting/EJB/WebLogic/WebSphere classes such as org.springframework.remoting.*, AbstractSlsbInvokerInterceptor, LocalSlsbInvokerInterceptor, JndiRmiServiceExporter, RemoteInvocation, RmiClientInterceptorUtils, WebLogicLoadTimeWeaver, WebSphereLoadTimeWeaver, and WebSphereMBeanServerFactoryBean. If tests exercise default @Configuration class enhancement/proxyBeanMethods=true in native image, set spring.objenesis.ignore=true for JVM/native test execution and either pre-generate the Spring CGLIB proxy classes before native-image compilation or prefer @Configuration(proxyBeanMethods=false)/GenericApplicationContext tests that avoid runtime CGLIB generation.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8719 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.springframework:spring-context:6.0.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Spring Context has many optional integrations; adding tests for Jakarta annotations, EJB, validation, Kotlin, Groovy, AspectJ, or reactive APIs may require targeted optional test dependencies, but they are not required for meaningful core ApplicationContext coverage.
Runtime CGLIB generation from default @Configuration classes can fail under native image unless handled explicitly.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is indicated; the javac failure is from stale Spring 5.x tests referencing APIs removed from spring-context 6.x, while normal Spring Context usage is in-process.
